Here's a comprehensive answer of what pgcli actually loads from the default `pgclirc` at startup, what it consumes, and what's inert.

---

## Configuration Keys Actually Consumed by pgcli

All keys below are read from the `[main]` section of `pgclirc` in `PGCli.__init__` (main.py) unless otherwise noted.

| Key | Default in `pgclirc` | Type Used | How Consumed |
|---|---|---|---|
| `smart_completion` | `True` | `as_bool` | Controls context-sensitive vs. full completion |
| `auto_suggest` | `True` | `as_bool` | Enables Fish-shell-style history auto-suggestions |
| `wider_completion_menu` | `False` | `as_bool` | Switches completion to multi-column layout |
| `always_use_single_connection` | `False` | `as_bool` | Forces single-connection mode for completion refresh |
| `multi_line` | `False` | `as_bool` | Toggles multi-line input mode |
| `multi_line_mode` | `psql` | `get` (str) | Controls Enter-key behavior in multi-line mode (`psql` or `safe`) |
| `destructive_warning` | `drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update` | `as_list` | List of SQL prefixes that trigger a confirmation warning |
| `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` | `False` | `as_bool` | If True, cancels & restarts the connection on Ctrl-C during a destructive statement |
| `destructive_statements_require_transaction` | `False` | `as_bool` | If True, blocks destructive statements outside a transaction |
| `expand` | `False` | `as_bool` | Toggles expanded (vertical) output mode (`\x` equivalent) |
| `auto_expand` | `False` | `as_bool` | Toggles auto-expanded output (`\x auto` equivalent) |
| `auto_retry_closed_connection` | `True` | `as_bool` | Auto-reconnects and re-runs on connection failure |
| `generate_aliases` | `False` | `as_bool` | Generates table aliases for completion |
| `alias_map_file` | *(empty)* | `get` (str, then `or None`) | Path to a JSON file mapping table names to specific aliases |
| `log_file` | `default` | `get` (str) | Log file path (`default` → `~/.config/pgcli/log`) |
| `keyword_casing` | `auto` | `get` (str) | Keyword casing: `lower`, `upper`, or `auto` |
| `casing_file` | `default` | `get` (str, via `get_casing_file()`) | Path to learned identifier casing file (`default` → `~/.config/pgcli/casing`) |
| `generate_casing_file` | `False` | `as_bool` | Whether to auto-generate the casing file from usage |
| `case_column_headers` | `True` | `as_bool` | Apply casing file to column headers in output |
| `history_file` | `default` | `get` (str) | Prompt history file (`default` → `~/.config/pgcli/history`) |
| `log_level` | `INFO` | `get` (str) | Python logging level (`CRITICAL`/`ERROR`/`WARNING`/`INFO`/`DEBUG`/`NONE`) |
| `asterisk_column_order` | `table_order` | `get` (str) | Order of columns when expanding `*`: `table_order` or `alphabetic` |
| `qualify_columns` | `if_more_than_one_table` | `get` (str) | Column qualification: `always`, `never`, `if_more_than_one_table` |
| `search_path_filter` | `False` | `as_bool` | When True, only suggests objects in `search_path` when no schema is specified |
| `timing` | `True` | `as_bool` | Enables display of query execution time |
| `hide_named_query_text` | `False` | `as_bool` | Hides query text when executing named queries (`\n <name>`) |
| `show_bottom_toolbar` | `True` | `as_bool` | Shows/hides the function-key toolbar |
| `table_format` | `psql` | `get` (str) | Output table format (e.g., `psql`, `grid`, `csv`, etc.) |
| `syntax_style` | `default` | `get` (str) | Pygments syntax-highlighting style name |
| `vi` | `False` | `as_bool` | Toggles Vi vs. Emacs keybindings |
| `on_error` | `STOP` | `get` (str, `.upper()`) | Error handling: `STOP` or `RESUME` |
| `row_limit` | `1000` | `as_int` | Row limit for SELECT output (0 = no limit) |
| `max_field_width` | `500` | `get` (str, then parsed as int) | Truncation width for long text fields (empty or `none` = no truncation) |
| `less_chatty` | `False` | `as_bool` | Suppresses the startup banner and goodbye message |
| `verbose_errors` | `False` | `as_bool` | Shows all PostgreSQL error diagnostic fields |
| `prompt` | `'\u@\h:\d> '` | `get` (str) | Prompt format string with `\u`, `\h`, `\d`, etc. placeholders |
| `min_num_menu_lines` | `4` | `as_int` | Number of terminal lines reserved for the suggestion menu |
| `multiline_continuation_char` | `''` (empty string) | `get` (str) | Character used for left-padding multi-line continuations |
| `null_string` | `<null>` | `get` (str) | Display string for NULL values |
| `enable_pager` | `True` | `as_bool` | Enables pager on startup |
| `keyring` | `True` | `as_bool` | Enables keyring-based password storage |
| `use_local_timezone` | `True` | `as_bool` | Sets session timezone to local (read in `cli()` in main.py, not in `__init__`) |

### Non-`[main]` sections also consumed

| Section | Key(s) | How Consumed |
|---|---|---|
| `[colors]` | All keys | Loaded as `c["colors"]` and passed to `style_factory()` / `style_factory_output()` in pgstyle.py, and used as `self.cli_style` |
| `[data_formats]` | `decimal`, `float` | Read as `c["data_formats"]["decimal"]` / `c["data_formats"]["float"]` for numeric formatting |
| `[column_date_formats]` | Any per-column strftime patterns | Loaded as `c["column_date_formats"]` and passed to cli_helpers for date formatting |
| `[ssh tunnels]` | Regex → tunnel URL pairs | Loaded as `c.get("ssh tunnels")` for host-based SSH tunnel configuration |
| `[dsn ssh tunnels]` | Regex → tunnel URL pairs | Loaded as `c.get("dsn ssh tunnels")` for DSN-alias-based SSH tunnel configuration |
| `[named queries]` | Named query definitions | Consumed indirectly through `NamedQueries.from_config(self.config_writer)` (pgspecial) |
| `[alias_dsn]` | DSN alias definitions | Read in `cli()` to resolve `--dsn` aliases |
| `[init-commands]` | Global init SQL | Read in `cli()` and executed after connecting |
| `[alias_dsn.init-commands]` | Per-DSN init SQL | Read in `cli()` and executed after connecting |

---

## Inert Keys (in `pgclirc` but NOT read anywhere in `pgcli/`)

The entire **`[audit]`** section is **inert**:

| Key | Default in `pgclirc` | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `[audit] enabled` | `True` | **Inert** — no code in `pgcli/` references `audit`, `redact_passwords`, `session_id_prefix`, or `audit.log_path` |
| `[audit] log_path` | `~/.config/pgcli/audit.log` | **Inert** |
| `[audit] redact_passwords` | `True` | **Inert** |
| `[audit] session_id_prefix` | `pgcli-` | **Inert** |

The `pgclirc` file itself notes this: *"an environment without the audit subsystem (e.g., a pip-installed checkout) treats these keys as no-ops"*. Indeed, searching the entire `pgcli/` directory for `audit`, `redact_passwords`, `session_id_prefix`, or `log_path` (outside of `main`'s own `log_file`) returns zero hits. These keys exist only for an external audit-maintained packaging that would overlay additional functionality.

---

### Notes on `pager`

The `pager` key in `[main]` is commented out by default in `pgclirc` (`# pager = less`), but the code in `set_default_pager()` does check for `config["main"].get("pager")` — so it **is consumed if uncommented**. It just has no active default value in the shipped config file.
